programme.title: Animals Like Us
programme.desc: Discover why it takes a village to raise lion cubs in Kenya and sniff out the mating strategy of fragrant lemurs in Madagascar. Cut a rug at a honeybee dance party, then listen up as a forked-tailed drongo tricks other birds out of their dinner.

credits.director: Kirstie McLure: Producer, Kait Borsay: Narrator

programme.title: Malawi Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: Feel heartbreak when Dr. Amanda’s team loses a pangolin abused by wildlife traffickers, but cheer when another is released post-rehab. Check on a trio of hedgehogs and tag along as the center’s baboons and nyala antelopes get a change of scenery.

credits.director: Jo Robinson:Producer

programme.title: Malawi Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: Strap in for Dr. Amanda’s risky attempt to collar a wild roan antelope. Later, chase down blue monkey Indigo while unrest ensues among the vervets. Examine an eagle owl with bumblefoot and say adieu to Sheila the croc, who’s headed back to the wild.

credits.director: Jo Robinson:Producer

programme.title: City Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: Join the rescue team as they respond to a call about an opossum stuck in a fence that turns out not to be the kind of animal that plays dead. Inside the center, a goose with a serious foot injury receives physiotherapy before returning to the wild.

credits.director: Stacey Hatch, David Hatch, Sharone Ostrovsky:Producers

programme.title: Malawi Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: Feel heartbreak when Dr. Amanda’s team loses a pangolin abused by wildlife traffickers, but cheer when another is released post-rehab. Check on a trio of hedgehogs and tag along as the center’s baboons and nyala antelopes get a change of scenery.

credits.director: Jo Robinson:Producer

programme.title: Malawi Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: Strap in for Dr. Amanda’s risky attempt to collar a wild roan antelope. Later, chase down blue monkey Indigo while unrest ensues among the vervets. Examine an eagle owl with bumblefoot and say adieu to Sheila the croc, who’s headed back to the wild.

credits.director: Jo Robinson:Producer

programme.title: Dawn to Dusk
programme.desc: Meet the unique primates and reptiles searching for water and dodging predators, from ring-tailed lemurs in the east coast rainforests to tiny brown leaf chameleons, in this land of extreme climate variations and habitats.

credits.director: Lorne Townsend: Series Producer

programme.title: Humanlike: The Chimps of Fongoli
programme.desc: Join anthropologist Jill Pruetz and guide Dondo (Johnny) Kanté and watch evolution happen in real time. Meet the elusive chimps of Fongoli, Senegal, who use tools, interact with fire, and adapt to the human world as it collides with their own.

credits.director: Oliver Twinch: Director, David Allen, Oliver Twinch: Producers

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: Travel to the ends of the Earth, where a drama is unfolding in real time. Meet animals, from tiny butterflies to huge humpback whales, that are acting as sentinels, adapting their behaviour, and helping us unlock the riddles of our changing planet.

credits.director: Shane Campbell-Stanton: Narrator; David Allen, Oliver Twitch: Producers

programme.title: Baboons: Rules of the Troop
programme.desc: Follow four families of four different species - the resourceful Beach Troop, the warlike Desert Troop, the disciplined River Troop, and the peaceful Mountain Band - to find out why these primates are among the most successful animals in Africa.

credits.director: Charlie Luckock: Producer

programme.title: Mysteries of the Mekong
programme.desc: The Mekong Delta is one of the largest on Earth. Formed over the last 8000 years, it fans out and discharges into the South China Sea via nine channels. These channels split and shape the coastline, creating unique habitats for a variety of animals.

credits.director: Leila Monks:Producer

programme.title: Malawi Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: Feel heartbreak when Dr. Amanda’s team loses a pangolin abused by wildlife traffickers, but cheer when another is released post-rehab. Check on a trio of hedgehogs and tag along as the center’s baboons and nyala antelopes get a change of scenery.

credits.director: Jo Robinson:Producer

programme.title: Malawi Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: Strap in for Dr. Amanda’s risky attempt to collar a wild roan antelope. Later, chase down blue monkey Indigo while unrest ensues among the vervets. Examine an eagle owl with bumblefoot and say adieu to Sheila the croc, who’s headed back to the wild.

credits.director: Jo Robinson:Producer

programme.title: Animals Like Us
programme.desc: Marvel at the toothy fish that help sharks maintain their pearly whites and discover the signature calls of dolphin families. Examine capuchin monkey’s nut-cracking stone tools, get the warm fuzzies when giraffes snuggle, and meet a meerkat mobster.

credits.director: Kirstie McLure: Producer, Kait Borsay: Narrator

programme.title: Humanlike: The Chimps of Fongoli
programme.desc: Join anthropologist Jill Pruetz and guide Dondo (Johnny) Kanté and watch evolution happen in real time. Meet the elusive chimps of Fongoli, Senegal, who use tools, interact with fire, and adapt to the human world as it collides with their own.

credits.director: Oliver Twinch: Director, David Allen, Oliver Twinch: Producers

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: Travel to the ends of the Earth, where a drama is unfolding in real time. Meet animals, from tiny butterflies to huge humpback whales, that are acting as sentinels, adapting their behaviour, and helping us unlock the riddles of our changing planet.

credits.director: Shane Campbell-Stanton: Narrator; David Allen, Oliver Twitch: Producers

programme.title: Baboons: Rules of the Troop
programme.desc: Follow four families of four different species - the resourceful Beach Troop, the warlike Desert Troop, the disciplined River Troop, and the peaceful Mountain Band - to find out why these primates are among the most successful animals in Africa.

credits.director: Charlie Luckock: Producer

programme.title: Mysteries of the Mekong
programme.desc: The Mekong Delta is one of the largest on Earth. Formed over the last 8000 years, it fans out and discharges into the South China Sea via nine channels. These channels split and shape the coastline, creating unique habitats for a variety of animals.

credits.director: Leila Monks:Producer

programme.title: Malawi Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: Feel heartbreak when Dr. Amanda’s team loses a pangolin abused by wildlife traffickers, but cheer when another is released post-rehab. Check on a trio of hedgehogs and tag along as the center’s baboons and nyala antelopes get a change of scenery.

credits.director: Jo Robinson:Producer

programme.title: Shadowlands
programme.desc: Learn how winter’s arrival triggers animal survival instincts in southwestern New Zealand. Track dolphins as they move to warmer waters to give their calves a fighting chance, and join longfin eels on an epic migration to their spawning grounds.

credits.director: Judith Curran: Developed By, Lemuel Lyes: Senior Archive Producer

programme.title: Dawn to Dusk
programme.desc: Meet the unique primates and reptiles searching for water and dodging predators, from ring-tailed lemurs in the east coast rainforests to tiny brown leaf chameleons, in this land of extreme climate variations and habitats.

credits.director: Lorne Townsend: Series Producer

programme.title: Humanlike: The Chimps of Fongoli
programme.desc: Join anthropologist Jill Pruetz and guide Dondo (Johnny) Kanté and watch evolution happen in real time. Meet the elusive chimps of Fongoli, Senegal, who use tools, interact with fire, and adapt to the human world as it collides with their own.

credits.director: Oliver Twinch: Director, David Allen, Oliver Twinch: Producers

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: Travel to the ends of the Earth, where a drama is unfolding in real time. Meet animals, from tiny butterflies to huge humpback whales, that are acting as sentinels, adapting their behaviour, and helping us unlock the riddles of our changing planet.

credits.director: Shane Campbell-Stanton: Narrator; David Allen, Oliver Twitch: Producers

programme.title: Baboons: Rules of the Troop
programme.desc: Follow four families of four different species - the resourceful Beach Troop, the warlike Desert Troop, the disciplined River Troop, and the peaceful Mountain Band - to find out why these primates are among the most successful animals in Africa.

credits.director: Charlie Luckock: Producer

programme.title: Mysteries of the Mekong
programme.desc: The Mekong Delta is one of the largest on Earth. Formed over the last 8000 years, it fans out and discharges into the South China Sea via nine channels. These channels split and shape the coastline, creating unique habitats for a variety of animals.

credits.director: Leila Monks:Producer

programme.title: Malawi Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: Feel heartbreak when Dr. Amanda’s team loses a pangolin abused by wildlife traffickers, but cheer when another is released post-rehab. Check on a trio of hedgehogs and tag along as the center’s baboons and nyala antelopes get a change of scenery.

credits.director: Jo Robinson:Producer

programme.title: Malawi Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: Strap in for Dr. Amanda’s risky attempt to collar a wild roan antelope. Later, chase down blue monkey Indigo while unrest ensues among the vervets. Examine an eagle owl with bumblefoot and say adieu to Sheila the croc, who’s headed back to the wild.

credits.director: Jo Robinson:Producer

programme.title: Animals Like Us
programme.desc: Marvel at the toothy fish that help sharks maintain their pearly whites and discover the signature calls of dolphin families. Examine capuchin monkey’s nut-cracking stone tools, get the warm fuzzies when giraffes snuggle, and meet a meerkat mobster.

credits.director: Kirstie McLure: Producer, Kait Borsay: Narrator

programme.title: Humanlike: The Chimps of Fongoli
programme.desc: Join anthropologist Jill Pruetz and guide Dondo (Johnny) Kanté and watch evolution happen in real time. Meet the elusive chimps of Fongoli, Senegal, who use tools, interact with fire, and adapt to the human world as it collides with their own.

credits.director: Oliver Twinch: Director, David Allen, Oliver Twinch: Producers

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: Travel to the ends of the Earth, where a drama is unfolding in real time. Meet animals, from tiny butterflies to huge humpback whales, that are acting as sentinels, adapting their behaviour, and helping us unlock the riddles of our changing planet.

credits.director: Shane Campbell-Stanton: Narrator; David Allen, Oliver Twitch: Producers

programme.title: City Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: Follow an injured opossum into surgery, as the medical team realizes amputation may be the only way to save his badly injured toe. And meet some turtle hatchlings who seem ready for release when the team gets disappointing details about their size.

credits.director: Stacey Hatch, David Hatch, Sharone Ostrovsky:Producers

programme.title: Australia's Hidden Islands
programme.desc: Christmas Island, over a thousand kilometres northwest of Australia’s mainland, is a speck of an island that rises out of the Indian Ocean, a tiny refuge to plants and creatures found nowhere else in the world. Birds rule its skies and it’s stunning landscapes are the stage for a miracle migration of millions.

credits.director: Michael Tear, Bettina Dalton:Producers

programme.title: Great Blue Wild
programme.desc: The coast of Mozambique is home to giants, like humpbacks and whale sharks, but the tiniest species play just as big of a role in the ecosystem. Meet the scientists who are working against the clock to protect coastal creatures big and small.

credits.director: Dan Hughes:Director

programme.title: Monkey Island
programme.desc: The jungle of Sri Lanka is a monkey’s paradise. But not everyone here is equal. Anna sits at the bottom of the social ladder. And with a newborn baby, life is getting tougher. As drought grips the region tensions boil over. Anna is a devoted mother. But can she do enough to keep her son alive.

credits.director: Martha Holmes, James Smith, Steven Clarke, Kate Beetham, Hannah Hoare:Producers

programme.title: Monkey Island
programme.desc: An ancient citadel once home to Sri Lanka’s kings has now been reclaimed by the jungle… and there’s a new dynasty in power. Monkeys.

credits.director: Martha Holmes, James Smith, Steven Clarke, Kate Beetham, Hannah Hoare:Producers

programme.title: Monkey Island
programme.desc: Tensions rise when drought overtakes an ancient Sri Lankan temple shared by tufted grey langurs, toque macaques, and purple-faced leaf monkeys. Witness the drama as the lower-ranking primates struggle to stay hydrated amid a mutinous plot.

credits.director: Martha Holmes, James Smith, Steven Clarke, Kate Beetham, Hannah Hoare:Producers

programme.title: Shark Squad
programme.desc: Off Guadalupe Island, Mexico, top shark ecologist Mauricio Hoyos attempts a television first. Get a unique view as he attaches a camera to the dorsal fin of a great white, revealing how it swims, hunts, and ultimately strikes its prey.

credits.director: Dan Hughes:Director

programme.title: Shark Squad
programme.desc: Travel to a remote part of the Bahamas to unlock shark secrets. Do they make friends? Where do they give birth? Are some bolder than others? The answers may help protect these supreme predators for generations to come.

credits.director: Dan Hughes:Director

programme.title: Zambia Untamed
programme.desc: In the Luangwa Valley lies a grove of Ebony trees, home to a troop of baboons. But, there are dangers here – the resident leopard who, unlike the other predators, is powerful and agile enough to climb trees. He’s not an easy foe to elude.

credits.director: Martha Holmes, James Smith, Kate Beetham, Hannah Hoare:Producers

programme.title: Zambia Untamed
programme.desc: The plains and woodlands of the Luangwa Valley are the wildest and most merciless of all neighborhoods where duels between predator and prey are played out both day and night. For the animals that call this home, it’s a daily battle to stay alive.

credits.director: Martha Holmes, James Smith, Kate Beetham, Hannah Hoare:Producers

programme.title: Zambia Untamed
programme.desc: As the dry season begins, thousands of animals are making their way to the Acacia Loop. Each must establish their place in the pecking order and avoid the dangerous residents who are all too willing to make a meal of the unwary.

credits.director: Martha Holmes, James Smith, Kate Beetham, Hannah Hoare:Producers

programme.title: Zambia Untamed
programme.desc: Mvula is the youngest calf in the Madzi family. Temperatures are soaring. Lagoons and waterholes are drying up. This forces Mvula and his family to undertake a long and dangerous journey to the only remaining source of water: the Luangwa River.

credits.director: Martha Holmes, James Smith, Kate Beetham, Hannah Hoare:Producers

programme.title: Baboons: Rules of the Troop
programme.desc: Follow four families of four different species - the resourceful Beach Troop, the warlike Desert Troop, the disciplined River Troop, and the peaceful Mountain Band - to find out why these primates are among the most successful animals in Africa.

credits.director: Charlie Luckock: Producer

programme.title: Wild Sky
programme.desc: Scan the Australian skies, which regularly deliver some of the world’s most volatile weather. Await cooling monsoon rains, watch lightning ignite vast wildfires, and marvel at the ingenious adaptations that help wildlife deal with these extremes.

credits.director: CHADDEN HUNTER: Producer, BRENNAN NURTHEN: Director

programme.title: Wild Sky
programme.desc: Experience the powerful, colourful phenomena that swirl across the heavens in North America. Watch polar bears search for food under the aurora borealis, map the frigid path of a polar vortex, and elude violent tornadoes with a prairie dog family.

credits.director: CHADDEN HUNTER: Producer, BRENNAN NURTHEN: Director

programme.title: Wild Sky
programme.desc: Observe the eternal search for water that defines the lives of Africa’s wild animals. Witness the biggest marine feeding frenzy on Earth, see how cobras stock up on solar power, and join wildebeest herds and elephant clans on their epic migrations.

credits.director: CHADDEN HUNTER: Producer, BRENNAN NURTHEN: Director

programme.title: Airborne
programme.desc: Watch beginners make their first leap into the air, like an Andean condor who must attend flight school before spreading its wings. Meet oddballs who soar without feathers and marvel at a peregrine falcon’s speed as it dives to catch its prey.

credits.director: Sally Cryer, Adam Simon, Dani Godwin: Producer/Director, Keeley Hawes: Narrator

programme.title: Airborne
programme.desc: Go hunting with flyers who excel at making mid-air catches. Watch the metamorphosis of the acrobatic emperor dragonfly, admire the barn owl’s stealthy approach to prey, and catch a wandering salamander’s death-defying leap into the abyss.

credits.director: Sally Cryer, Adam Simon, Dani Godwin: Producer/Director, Keeley Hawes: Narrator

programme.title: Airborne
programme.desc: Check in with animals who are winners at the aerial mating game. Take flight with a bee crafting a custom love potion, watch a draco lizard soar to fend off rivals, and witness the graceful pas de deux that bonds a pair of Japanese cranes for life.

credits.director: Sally Cryer, Adam Simon, Dani Godwin: Producer/Director, Keeley Hawes: Narrator

programme.title: Airborne
programme.desc: Plot a course for adventure as animals go on remarkable journeys through the skies. Navigate a wooden labyrinth with a speedy goshawk, join a sandgrouse’s trek to a distant watering hole, and see a murmuration of starlings mesmerize their nemesis.

credits.director: Sally Cryer, Adam Simon, Dani Godwin: Producer/Director, Keeley Hawes: Narrator

programme.title: Wild Sky
programme.desc: Scan the Australian skies, which regularly deliver some of the world’s most volatile weather. Await cooling monsoon rains, watch lightning ignite vast wildfires, and marvel at the ingenious adaptations that help wildlife deal with these extremes.

credits.director: CHADDEN HUNTER: Producer, BRENNAN NURTHEN: Director

programme.title: Wild Sky
programme.desc: Experience the powerful, colourful phenomena that swirl across the heavens in North America. Watch polar bears search for food under the aurora borealis, map the frigid path of a polar vortex, and elude violent tornadoes with a prairie dog family.

credits.director: CHADDEN HUNTER: Producer, BRENNAN NURTHEN: Director

programme.title: Wild Sky
programme.desc: Observe the eternal search for water that defines the lives of Africa’s wild animals. Witness the biggest marine feeding frenzy on Earth, see how cobras stock up on solar power, and join wildebeest herds and elephant clans on their epic migrations.

credits.director: CHADDEN HUNTER: Producer, BRENNAN NURTHEN: Director

programme.title: Airborne
programme.desc: Watch beginners make their first leap into the air, like an Andean condor who must attend flight school before spreading its wings. Meet oddballs who soar without feathers and marvel at a peregrine falcon’s speed as it dives to catch its prey.

credits.director: Sally Cryer, Adam Simon, Dani Godwin: Producer/Director, Keeley Hawes: Narrator
